assessor_1_raw: The design is methodologically sound. The transformer and baseline are fairly compared with identical feature access (baseline features + sequence for transformer). PR-AUC is the right metric. Secondary metrics are comprehensive. Temporal split is appropriate. No flaws. Verdict: defense_wins.
assessor_2_raw: Well-designed escalation prediction experiment. The operational label definition, temporal split, and equal tuning budget are all appropriate. The agent leakage prevention and product-line stratification are good confound controls. ECE secondary metric addresses calibration concerns. Verdict: defense_wins.
assessor_3_raw: The experiment correctly tests whether sequence modeling adds value over metadata+BoW. Both models have access to identical features; the transformer has additional sequence information. PR-AUC with bootstrap CIs is appropriate. The scope is honestly bounded. No methodological flaws found. Verdict: defense_wins.
adjudication_raw: All three assessors unanimous: defense_wins. The experiment is consistently evaluated as methodologically sound across all runs.
